howItWorks.intro: This rare earth mineral supply disruption calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Supply gap = restriction % × China market share %
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Has China ever restricted rare earth exports?
faq.answer: Yes. In 2010, China cut rare earth exports to Japan by 40% during a territorial dispute. Prices spiked 300-600%. The WTO ruled against China in 2014, but China still controls the processing infrastructure.

faq.question: Is the Rare Earth Mineral Supply Disruption Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard geopolitics and energy markets formulas trusted by analysts and informed citizens. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What export restriction level should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate export restriction level value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Rare Earth Mineral Supply Disruption Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Rare Earth Mineral Supply Disruption Calculator?
sections.body: The Rare Earth Mineral Supply Disruption Calculator is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your export restriction level, focus sector, you get instant results including supply gap, price impact, sector impact. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting supply gap right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your export restriction level and need to find the right supply gap. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Export Restriction Level
howToSteps.description: Type or select your export restriction level in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Focus Sector
howToSteps.description: Type or select your focus sector in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your supply gap, price impact, sector impact. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your export restriction level before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both supply gap and price impact — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to understand global dynamics.
If you're unsure about your focus sector, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
